Houses.traditional_name: House of the Querent (Helm / House of Self)
governs: The querent—the person asking the question
Personality, consciousness, and the physical body
Any vehicle in which the querent is traveling
Location and weather where the querent is situated
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: Never the House of the Quesited in standard house-chart work. It anchors the chart as the querent’s own seat.
Houses.notes: Mercury rejoices in the helm: speech, carriage, and the pattern of the breath steer outcomes in quiet, bodily ways. A martial figure often marks blunt self-assertion; a lunar tail frequently means the querent has already rehearsed a verdict before sitting down. Read the First as the live feed—temperament visible in posture—not as a costume chosen for the consultation.
Houses.interpretive_essence: The First is the querent as instrument: pulse, posture, and the story offered from the chair. It does not hold the quesited in classical practice; it colors every other house with disposition and pace.
key_significators: Name, bearing, and reputation at a glance
common_misreadings: Placing a spouse in the First when the matter clearly belongs to the Seventh
Treating Rubeus only as moral evil rather than heat, friction, or secrecy
Reading Populus as absence instead of echo, multitude, or deferral
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: When the First shows a passive multitude while ally houses flare, ask whether the narrative is curated for witnesses. Pair angular Mars figures with cadent Albus to see whether anger is being cooled—or concealed—by counsel.

governs: Investments that behave like savings or cash flow (excluding speculative plays and real-property titles)
Body zones tied to intake: mouth, jaw, throat, and food consumed
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns everyday finances, gains or losses, theft, or personal belongings.
Houses.notes: Liquidity lives here: what moves across the table. Land titles lean toward the Fourth; venture or wager tilt toward the Fifth. Classical authors watched conjunctions to the Second when cash or goods were literally changing hands.
example_questions: Will my business make or lose money?
Will the investment be profitable?
Will I get paid for the work I did?
Is this a good time to borrow money?
Is the antique table worth what the seller is asking?
Will the package I shipped arrive safely?
Has my missing watch been stolen?
Will my stolen car be recovered?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Cash flow, movable goods, and the felt sense of enoughness. The Second answers whether resources arrive, depart, or linger in transit—not whether a life’s meaning is secure.
key_significators: Mouth and throat as intake—literal and metaphorical
common_misreadings: Routing monthly rent through the Second when the lease itself is a Fourth-house matter
Labeling leveraged crypto as Second when it behaves like Fifth-house risk
Overlooking theft or loss as legitimate Second themes
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Amissio on the Second with Acquisitio in the Fifth can show money dancing through risk and back—follow the sequence of houses, not the snapshot of a single figure.

governs: Journeys under roughly two hundred miles—daily commutes and regional hops
Education from preschool through high school, and ordinary skill-building
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns siblings or cousins (not parents), neighbors, the near environment, media, rumor, or early schooling.
Houses.notes: The Moon rejoices among letters, cousins, and short wheels. News arrives in bundles—messages, notices, portals—not only in formal documents. Discern signal from noise before you build strategy on chatter.
example_questions: Will my relationship with my sister improve?
Will the neighbors agree to an easement?
Is this a good weekend to drive to the beach?
Should I enroll my child in North Central Elementary?
Is my neighbor George's advice worth following?
Is the news story accurate?
Should I believe the rumors?
Houses.interpretive_essence: The immediate circuit: kin nearby, neighbors, short trips, and the rumor ecology that feeds or unsettles decisions.
common_misreadings: Elevating every group chat to the Third when the stress is vocational or public-tenth
Confusing ninth-house philosophy or pilgrimage with neighborhood friction
Treating rumor as fact without corroboration
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Conjunctio in the Third with Carcer in the Ninth can show paperwork blocked upstream while local talk runs free—address the choke point, not only the noise.

governs: Land, real estate, and physical infrastructure
Hidden objects, ancient sites, and antiquities
The querent’s father, parents in general, and ancestry
The final outcome of any matter (Significator of Completion)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns place, real estate, farming, lineage, endings, or completion. It also holds the Significator of Completion in house-chart method.
Houses.notes: Angular earth: foundations, closure, fathers, and the voice that pronounces how a matter lands when dust settles. Many lineages let the Fourth speak last when other houses have argued their parts.
example_questions: Is the property worth buying?
Is the soil fertile?
Will roses flourish here?
Should I move to another apartment?
Is Springfield a good place to relocate?
Is the table antique or a modern reproduction?
Is the pension fund in reliable hands?
How is my father doing?
How will the present situation turn out in the end?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Roots, soil, dwellings, and the structural finish of a story. Outcomes named here settle into beams and weathering, not applause.
common_misreadings: Using the Fourth for speculative instruments when the Fifth fits better
Calling every basement leak Twelfth when title, deed, or foundation is Fourth
Skipping the completion read when the question explicitly asks how something ends
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Fortuna Major in the Fourth with Via in the Tenth can show domestic stability underwriting a public pivot—weigh which angular message bears more literal weight for the query.

governs: Crops from perennial or biennial plants
Bodies of water, fishing, and rain
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns fertility, pleasure, creative pursuit, casual romance, children, or speculative risk.
Houses.notes: Venus rejoices where joy is cultivated: flirtation, seedlings, creative ventures, and wagers that need a spark. Pleasure here is not contract; contract belongs across the wheel.
example_questions: Will the grape crop be good this year?
Is Janis interested in me sexually?
Is my unborn child a boy or a girl?
How well will my four-year-old cope with a baby brother?
Will the concert be worth the ticket price?
Is next Friday a good night for the party?
Is the new Asian restaurant in town worth a visit?
Will the dress be ready in time for the wedding?
How will fishing be on the lake this weekend?
Will it rain tomorrow?
Will I receive a letter from my grandmother?
Are the claims in the book I'm reading accurate?
Houses.interpretive_essence: The economy of delight: romance without vows, fertility signals, creative flow, and stakes taken for the thrill as much as the return.
common_misreadings: Moving pregnancy significators away from their natural houses without textual reason
Reading Fifth romance as Seventh marriage or contract
Ignoring literal rain or water omens when the query is meteorological
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Puella in the Fifth with Rubeus in the Eighth can tie a flirtation to shared debt or obligation—read both timelines before you isolate romance.

governs: Service trades—physicians, plumbers, entertainers, and similar
Practitioners of magic or occult arts other than the querent
Pets and household-scale animals (not cattle-class livestock)
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns hired help, employees, pets, illness, injury, or routine maintenance.
Houses.notes: Mars rejoices in labor, small animals, and the body’s daily ledger. Illness here is disruption of rhythm and capacity; deeper transformation or shared crisis often speaks through the Eighth.
example_questions: Should I hire Phyllis?
Are my employees stealing from the till?
Are the plumbers reliable?
Should I hire a band for the party?
Will I find my lost dog?
Will hogs fetch a good price this fall?
How serious is this illness?
Would it be worthwhile to get a Tarot reading from Marie?
Is this a good time to have dental work done?
Will I catch the flu this winter?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Service loops: wages and tasks, clinicians and craftspeople, pets under the roof, and the calendar that keeps life runnable.
key_significators: Domestic animals up to the scale of hogs or goats
common_misreadings: Routing every burnout to the Twelfth without examining Sixth workload or staffing
Calling every practitioner Sixth when teaching or doctrine is ninth-house
Confusing acute presentation (often tied to First-body concerns) with chronic grind
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Carcer in the Sixth with Laetitia in the Twelfth can show grinding duty relieved by private respite—check sleep, boundaries, and off-duty recovery.

governs: Conflict, competition, lawsuits, and predator–prey dynamics
Hunting and locating a specified person
The physician in dedicated medical charts
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns deep one-to-one ties, formal agreements, open conflict, locating someone, or naming thieves.
Houses.notes: The descendant angle faces the other: lover, opponent, or signatory across the table. Contracts and contests share one geometry—two fronts on a single line.
example_questions: Does Stanley love me?
Will this relationship last?
Is this a good time to propose marriage?
Should Carol and I form a business partnership?
Should I sign the contract?
Will the Packers win the Super Bowl?
Will India and Pakistan sign a peace treaty?
Will my company's bid on the project be accepted?
Would it be to my advantage to file a lawsuit?
Will the police catch the thief?
Is next weekend a good time for deer hunting?
Will I be able to get in contact with Julie again?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Pair-bonds, declared rivals, and mirrored stakes. Seventh is where the querent meets what cannot be swallowed alone.
common_misreadings: Forcing a crush into the Seventh before reciprocity or obligation exists
Treating a sports contest as Seventh without a clear quesited mapping
Forgetting the physician-as-seventh convention in medical layouts
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Puer in the Seventh with Albus in the First can pit a sharp rival against a softly spoken querent—precision of terms matters more than volume.

governs: Death and matters contiguous with it
Apparitions, spiritual entities, and the occult as lived experience
Practical magic performed by or for the querent
Circumstances around absent or missing persons
Money, goods, or favors lent out
Inheritance, legacy, and jointly held resources
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns absence, deep change, death’s effects, missing persons, or lent and shared pools.
Houses.notes: The classical gate of shared depths: other people’s stakes, voids, and the operative workbench—distinct from ninth-house doctrine or teaching. Ethical readers decline lifespan prurience; transformation and obligation still belong here.
example_questions: Should I get life insurance?
How seriously should I take Bill's threats?
Is this a good time to make a will?
Is the house actually haunted?
Is the magical working I am planning appropriate?
Is my neighbor's missing daughter all right?
Will I ever get back the book I loaned to Greg?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Shared pools and absences: loans and legacies, what is missing, and craft undertaken at the altar rather than debated in the seminar hall.
key_significators: Hands-on spellcraft tied to the querent
common_misreadings: Parking university theology here simply because the topic feels uncanny
Trading in sensational death detail
Confusing fraud or petty theft (Second) with eighth-house shared obligation
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Cauda in the Eighth with Caput in the Second can mark closure of a debt opening a new cash chapter—honor order of events, not symbols in isolation.

governs: Long journeys over roughly two hundred miles by land
Voyage by water, air, or space
Dream interpretation when framed as inquiry
Occult philosophy, astrology, and divination as disciplines
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns long travel, spiritual seeking, higher education, publishing, or testing divinatory reliability.
Houses.notes: The Sun rejoices on long roads, in print, and in faith argued under open sky. Divination’s philosophy sits here; the working altar often answers from the Eighth.
example_questions: Will I be able to go to Germany this summer?
How congested will the airport be?
Should I pursue my interest in Pagan religion?
Will I be able to get into Harvard?
Is this a good time to go back to school and finish my degree?
Should I sign up for music lessons, or would I be wasting my time?
Does the dream I had last night mean anything?
Should I study geomancy?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Far horizons: pilgrimage and visa lines, faculty and clergy, and the maps—intellectual or sacred—we draw to explain the world.
key_significators: Dreams as texts when the question is interpretive
common_misreadings: Labeling every flight Ninth when the trip is a commute-third matter
Placing grimoire operations here without a doctrinal or teaching frame
Ignoring literal ocean passage when the chart demands voyage literalism
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Via in the Ninth with Populus in the Third can show study-abroad chatter running ahead of facts—verify channels before you advise.

governs: The querent’s mother (in many traditional schemes)
Politics from local boards to global bodies
Weather in collective or public scope
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns status, ambition, job change, governance, public weather, or the mother when that convention applies.
Houses.notes: Midheaven angle: headline name, boss or crown, collective weather. Mother significations travel here in numerous lineages beside career and mandate.
example_questions: Should I look for a different job?
Will I get the promotion?
Will I get along with my new boss?
Will the county office approve my building permit?
How is Mom doing these days?
Will Senator Smith win reelection?
Should I run for a seat on the school board?
Will the weather tomorrow be clear and dry?
Should I get a second opinion on the proposed treatment for my heart condition?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Public arc: title, politics, reputation, and the visible mother or authority mirror.
common_misreadings: Mixing personal brand (First) with institutional role (Tenth)
Placing mother only in the Fourth when the chart’s tradition uses Tenth
Reading a single tenth-house headline without relation to aspects or witnesses
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Fortuna Minor in the Tenth with Tristitia in the Fourth can show visible success stressing domestic foundations—negotiate bandwidth before accepting every spotlight.

Houses.astrological_correspondence: Social Life, Community, and Future Vision
governs: Promises, patrons, and sources of help
Questions the querent declines to state aloud
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns allies, networks, durable hopes, benefactors, annual harvest—or the true question is deliberately withheld.
Houses.notes: Jupiter rejoices among friends, benefactors, and the crop that closes the season. Hope sometimes masks the real ask; read kindly and verify what is actually needed.
example_questions: How is my old college buddy Bill doing?
Will my friend Sally make good on her promise?
Can I count on the neighborhood association to support me?
Will I achieve my fondest dream?
Will I get a good crop of peas this year?
I don't want to tell you what my question is—can you answer it anyway?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Networks and patrons, promises kept or strained, and the forward tilt of desire. Eleventh shows who arrives when the truck is stuck in the mud.
common_misreadings: Reading anonymous forums as Eleventh without a lived tie to the querent
Promising windfalls from Jupiter rhetoric alone
Ignoring strings attached to patronage
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Rubeus in the Eleventh with Populus in the First can show a volatile circle cheering a querent who remains inwardly undecided—test sincerity before leaning on the crowd.

governs: Secrets, concealed struggle, and the subconscious
Harmful magic attributed to another against the querent
Large livestock and wild animals—cattle, horses, donkeys, mules, camels
Houses.significator_of_quesited_when: The question concerns harm, strict limits, debt owed by the querent, hidden foes, large animals, or confinement.
Houses.notes: Saturn rejoices in what stays unseen: institutions, karmic ledgers, field-sized beasts, and rivals not yet introduced. Self-undoing is literal when we quietly dissolve our own calendar.
example_questions: Will I be able to pay my bills next month?
Will I be sent to prison?
Is Ruth hiding something from me?
Is someone at work trying to get me fired?
Was the poppet in the front yard a joke, or is someone trying to cast a spell on me?
Is this horse worth buying?
Will cattle fetch a good price this year?
Houses.interpretive_essence: Seclusion, herd-scale animals, suspicion of occult harm, and the subconscious leak that steers choices from below deck.
common_misreadings: Blaming the Twelfth for every anxiety when Sixth workload or health regimen explains the strain
Naming secret enemies without proportional evidence
Ignoring livestock markets when the query is ranch or herd economics
Houses.figure_combinations_to_watch: Laetitia in the Twelfth with Carcer in the Sixth can show relief inside constraint—a humane ward or structured care—read compassion through schedule and limits.
